name: Manufacturing Strategy Detection
description: A manufacturer's strategy — Make-to-Stock (MTS), Make-to-Order (MTO), or Engineer-to-Order (ETO) — determines the entire operating model: how demand is forecasted, how inventory is managed, how customer commitments are made. Applying MTS patterns to an ETO manufacturer (or vice versa) produces guidance that is worse than useless — it is actively harmful. This pattern detects the manufacturing strategy from the client profile and configures the appropriate strategy-specific pattern set. Kimre (the platform's reference implementation) is an ETO/MTO/MTS hybrid.
trigger: Client profile is loaded for a manufacturing industry client (industry code = MFG) OR client onboarding questionnaire is completed.
logic: 1. Read client profile manufacturing_strategy field (F22). 2. If field absent: run detection heuristic — check: custom engineering at quote stage (ETO signal), order-specific production scheduling (MTO signal), finished goods warehouse maintained (MTS signal). 3. Classify: ETO (dominant engineering and project work), MTO (production triggered by orders, minimal finished goods), MTS (production to forecast, finished goods buffer), or HYBRID (explicit combination, document split). 4. Set strategy context for session: ETO → enable project-based tracking patterns, MTO → enable order-milestone patterns, MTS → enable inventory-level monitoring patterns. 5. Log classification with evidence for CAiO review.
escalation: If strategy cannot be determined from available data: notify Process Owner and request clarification before applying any strategy-specific patterns.
ground_truth_check: Client Operations or Engineering lead confirms the detected strategy matches their actual production model. Any correction is a high-signal pattern candidate.
promotion_criteria: 30+ manufacturing clients classified. Classification confirmed by client in > 85% of cases. Hybrid detection logic validated against Kimre and similar complex manufacturers.

name: Material Shortage Escalation
description: Material shortages in manufacturing create a cascade: a missing BOM line item blocks fabrication, which delays delivery, which affects customer relationship. The earlier the escalation, the more options remain available (expedite, substitute, reschedule). This pattern creates the early warning structure. The critical constraint: auto-creation of Purchase Requisitions above £10K without approval is not permitted — the value threshold is the authority gate.
trigger: Material coverage check detects: any BOM line item on an active manufacturing order has coverage_days < 5 (based on current stock + open POs / average daily consumption).
logic: 1. Run daily material coverage check against active order BOM lines. 2. For each shortage detected: calculate required PR quantity (reorder point + safety stock - current coverage). 3. Calculate estimated PR value (quantity × standard cost). 4. If PR value <= £10K: generate PR draft, route to Purchasing for approval, notify Engineering Lead. 5. If PR value > £10K: escalate to Engineering Lead with recommended quantity and context — do NOT auto-create PR. 6. Include in escalation: item, order affected, days of coverage remaining, recommended qty, estimated cost, 2-3 potential suppliers from approved vendor list. 7. Log shortage event with timestamp and escalation chain.
escalation: Engineering Lead (primary, within 2h for < 3 days coverage). Purchasing Manager (concurrent for PR value > £10K). If no response in 4h: Production Manager. If order delivery date at risk: Customer Service Lead.
ground_truth_check: After escalation: did the Engineering Lead receive the alert before the shortage blocked production? Was the recommended PR quantity accurate? Did the order ship on time?
promotion_criteria: 30+ shortage escalations processed. Zero cases where shortage-to-production-block occurred after escalation was sent (indicates response process is working). PR quantity accuracy > 80%.

name: Quality CAPA Triage Pattern
description: Non-conformance reports and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) items require rapid triage to prevent quality escapes — where a defect reaches the customer. The severity classification determines the response timeline. Critical and high severity items must reach the Quality Manager within 4 hours; this is not a target, it is the line between an internal quality event and a customer quality event. This pattern automates the classification and routing, removing the bottleneck of manual triage.
trigger: A new Non-Conformance Report (NCR) or CAPA item is created in the quality management system.
logic: 1. Receive NCR/CAPA event. 2. Classify severity using rules: CRITICAL = (safety_impact = true) OR (regulatory_exposure = high) OR (customer_facing = true AND defect_rate > 5%); HIGH = (customer_facing = true) OR (production_line_blocked = true) OR (regulatory_exposure = medium); MEDIUM = (internal_rework_required = true) AND customer_facing = false; LOW = cosmetic or documentation issue only. 3. Route by severity: CRITICAL → Quality Manager + Compliance Officer immediately, Production stopped pending review. HIGH → Quality Manager within 4h. MEDIUM → Quality team queue, 48h review window. LOW → Auto-queue, 5-day review. 4. For LOW severity: if no escalation within 5 days, auto-close with standard resolution note. 5. Generate triage report for each item.
escalation: CRITICAL: Quality Manager + Production Manager + (if regulated product) Compliance Officer simultaneously. No auto-close for CRITICAL or HIGH. HIGH with no response in 4h: escalate to Operations Director.
ground_truth_check: Quality Manager confirms: severity classification was accurate, routing was correct, response time met target. Any reclassification by Quality Manager is a correction event.
promotion_criteria: 50+ NCR/CAPA items triaged. Classification accuracy confirmed by Quality Manager > 85%. Zero CRITICAL items that were initially misclassified as MEDIUM or LOW (this is the catastrophic failure mode).

name: OEE Threshold Alert
description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) below 75% is the industry-standard signal that a production line needs attention. A week-over-week decline of 5% or more signals accelerating deterioration — not just underperformance, but a trajectory that will get worse. This pattern creates the detection and response structure. Critical constraint: maintenance scheduling is not automated — Production Manager approval is required before any maintenance downtime is committed. Unplanned maintenance windows have their own cost.
trigger: Daily OEE calculation shows: (a) OEE < 75% for any production line, OR (b) week-over-week OEE delta <= -5 percentage points for any line.
logic: 1. Calculate daily OEE per line: Availability × Performance × Quality. 2. Calculate 7-day rolling average and week-over-week delta. 3. On trigger: identify which OEE component is driving the issue (Availability = downtime/changeover, Performance = speed loss, Quality = defect/rework). 4. Generate maintenance review request: line ID, OEE current vs. threshold, component breakdown, trend chart (7 days), probable cause hypothesis, recommended maintenance action. 5. Route to Production Manager for approval. 6. Do NOT schedule maintenance without Production Manager approval — scheduling has knock-on effects on order commitments. 7. Log alert with timestamp, OEE value, and component breakdown.
escalation: Production Manager (primary, within 4h for < 70% OEE). If OEE < 65%: immediate escalation to Operations Director. If trend shows 3 consecutive days of decline: escalate regardless of absolute level.
ground_truth_check: After maintenance action: did OEE recover to above threshold within 5 working days? If not, was the root cause diagnosis accurate? Production Manager feedback on recommendation quality.
promotion_criteria: 30+ OEE alerts processed. Root cause hypothesis accuracy > 70% (confirmed by maintenance team). Maintenance response time from alert to scheduled action < 48h average.

name: ETO Milestone Proactive Customer Notification
description: In Engineer-to-Order manufacturing, customers are heavily invested in delivery timelines — they may have their own project milestones, installation windows, or regulatory submissions that depend on the manufacturer's delivery. The worst customer experience is the customer who discovers a delay when they follow up, not when the manufacturer tells them proactively. This pattern creates the proactive notification structure. Authority is HIGH because outbound customer communications are consequential — a poorly worded delay notice can damage the relationship, create legal exposure, or trigger contract penalties.
trigger: ETO order: fabrication milestone actual date is > 2 days behind the scheduled date (either current milestone or projected impact on subsequent milestones).
logic: 1. Monitor ETO order milestone schedule daily. 2. Detect milestone delay >= 2 days against plan. 3. Calculate downstream impact: which subsequent milestones are affected, what is the projected new delivery date. 4. Draft customer notification: factual delay description, revised milestone schedule, root cause (one sentence, verified with Engineering), immediate actions being taken. 5. Draft must NOT include: promised recovery dates not confirmed by Engineering, blame attribution to supply chain without verification, guaranteed delivery date unless Operations Director has confirmed. 6. Route draft to Customer Service Lead for review and approval. 7. Do not send without explicit Customer Service approval. 8. Log notification draft with timestamp and routing.
escalation: Customer Service Lead (must approve all outbound communications). If delay > 10 days from original schedule: Operations Director must also approve. If contract penalty clauses may be triggered: route to commercial/legal for review before Customer Service approval.
ground_truth_check: After notification sent: customer acknowledged receipt, understood the delay, did not escalate as surprised by information they should have received earlier. Customer Service Lead confirms draft quality.
promotion_criteria: At maturity 2 (mechanical). Promotion to 3 requires: 30+ notifications drafted, < 20% requiring significant revision by Customer Service Lead, at least 2 cases where customer explicitly thanked proactive communication. CAiO review.

name: Compliance Certification Gate
description: Material certifications (mill certs, chemical analysis, third-party test reports) are the documentary foundation of quality compliance in manufacturing. Starting fabrication on materials without verified certifications creates risk at two points: quality (you may be using out-of-spec material) and compliance (you may not be able to prove conformance to a customer or auditor). Once fabrication starts, unraveling it is extremely costly. The gate must happen before work begins, not after. No workaround is acceptable without a documented exception — and the exception itself creates an audit trail.
trigger: Manufacturing order status changes to 'Ready for Fabrication Start' OR scheduled fabrication start date is T-1 working day.
logic: 1. Retrieve BOM for the order. 2. For each material line item: query certification records — check: (a) cert exists in document system, (b) cert type matches requirement (material spec, heat number, etc.), (c) cert is not expired (check expiry date for time-sensitive certs). 3. If ALL certs present and valid: GREEN status, fabrication start confirmed. 4. If ANY cert missing or expired: BLOCK fabrication start. 5. Alert Quality Manager and Production Planner: which material, which cert missing/expired, order ID, scheduled start date. 6. Fabrication start remains blocked until: (a) cert received and verified by Quality Manager, OR (b) documented exception approved by Quality Manager with rationale. 7. Log gate result: pass, block, or exception.
escalation: Quality Manager (immediate notification on block). If cert cannot be obtained within 24h of scheduled start: Production Manager + Engineering Lead to assess options (substitute material, expedite cert, reschedule order).
ground_truth_check: Zero fabrication starts on materials without verified certifications (the only acceptable outcome). Any exception must be traceable in audit log. Quality Manager confirms gate is functioning correctly.
promotion_criteria: 50+ order start gates processed. Zero fabrication starts without certification (hard requirement). Exception rate tracked and reviewed quarterly by Quality Manager. CAiO sign-off.
